title: Fishing crew rescued near Gloucester after boat runs aground amid harsh seas
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two crew members were rescued from the fishing boat Legacy after it ran aground near Gloucester on Saturday night.
First responders from Gloucester police, fire, and harbormaster coordinated with the US Coast Guard in the rescue.
The boat was stuck on rocks off Atlantic Road, facing 2 to 4-foot waves, causing hull damage but no injuries.

title: The Rafael Devers trade is already haunting the Red Sox in 2026
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox have moved on from key pieces received in the Rafael Devers trade with the Giants.
Boston is 2-7 to start the 2026 season, struggling without Devers and Alex Bregman in the lineup.
Prospect James Tibbs, traded to the Dodgers, is excelling in Triple-A while Red Sox lag offensively.

title: Why it might be time for a slumping Bruins team to call up James Hagens
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins scored only three goals in their last three games and are struggling offensively as playoffs near.
Prospect James Hagens could be called up from Providence Bruins to boost Boston's offense before the playoffs.
Hagens has four points in six AHL games and could strengthen Boston's power play and third line.

title: Tatum, Celtics pull away late to beat Raptors, move closer to 2-seed: 6 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: The Boston Celtics beat the Toronto Raptors 113-95 on Sunday, led by Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown.
Neemias Queta showcased strong performance with 18 points, making a case for the NBA's Most Improved Player award.
Celtics face the Hornets next at TD Garden, holding a three-game lead over the Knicks for the 2-seed.

title: New Bedford man gets decades-long prison sentence for raping young relative
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Daniel Turcios Torres, 46, received a 36 to 45-year prison sentence for raping a young relative in New Bedford.
The assaults occurred over five years, starting when the victim moved from Ecuador at age 12, the DA said.
Turcios Torres faces charges of rape, child rape by force, and intimidation, and will be subject to an ICE detainer.

title: She is running Boston in honor of her mother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Madison Oakes is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her mother who died of Stage 4 brain cancer.
Her mother lived three and a half years after a grand mal seizure, with treatment at MGB cancer center.
Oakes, now an oncology nurse, is participating for the second year on behalf of Massachusetts General Hospital.

title: Boston police fatally shoot suspect allegedly wielding sword near Northeastern University campus
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police shot and killed a man allegedly attacking with a sword during a mental health crisis near Northeastern University.
The suspect reportedly stabbed an officer and knocked an EMS clinician down before being shot by multiple officers.
The incident follows several other violent events in the Northeastern University area within the past two weeks.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her godmother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Abigail Dubois, 23, from Hudson, NH, is running the Boston Marathon to honor her godmother, Diana, who died of brain cancer.
Dubois qualified for the marathon at the 2025 Lake Erie Marathon and aims to support Boston's Cancer Care Equity program.
She is running with the Massachusetts General Hospital team to raise funds for families facing cancer treatment expenses.

title: 1 dead, 2 hospitalized in fatal Brockton house fire, officials say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A fire at 80 Glendale Ave., Brockton, resulted in one death and two hospitalizations on Saturday.
Firefighters found heavy flames on the second floor and extinguished the blaze after discovering a deceased woman.
Investigators determined the fire started in a second-floor bedroom but the exact cause remains unknown.

title: NH suspect who allegedly shot at family members killed in officer-involved shooting
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Masse, 38, was killed in a shootout with New Hampshire police Saturday night after firing at officers.
Masse allegedly shot a Nottingham officer earlier in the day after firing a long gun at family members.
Residents were asked to shelter in place as state police searched for Masse, who fled into the woods.
